signals.reasoning: BTC is stable with mild trend-supported mechanics, but the active veto blocks chasing a fresh long and there is no clean short edge either.
ETH still looks structurally firm with positive price and open-interest support, but under veto it remains watch-only rather than a fresh long.
SOL reads more like a short-covering bounce than a clean trend, which makes it unattractive in either direction under the event brake.
This is one of the cleaner crowded-long fades on the board: price is up hard, open interest is expanding with the move, and funding is positive enough to make the upside look crowded.
STBL remains the clearest bearish fade because funding is still extremely positive, crowding is elevated, and the rally already looks expensive rather than fresh.
XPL is strong but not clean enough for a veto-breaking long, and there is not enough crowding to justify a fresh short either.
WLD has upside momentum, but the signal quality is only moderate and this is still a veto regime, so I would keep it watch-only.
Liquidity is too thin and spreads are too wide for a serious new position here, especially with the event brake active.
ENA is one of the few names showing an early rollover profile without already being fully broken, so it is a reasonable secondary short candidate if we need one.
FOGO already looks like a flush rather than a fresh breakdown, so pressing the short here would be late.
VVV is strong and extended, but veto keeps it in watch-only territory rather than a chase long.
